items.q: Does a sea wall stop coastal erosion for good?
items.correct: No — sea walls reflect wave energy, need costly maintenance, can accelerate beach scour at their base, and may starve places downdrift of sediment, shifting the problem along the coast.
items.misconception: Hard engineering permanently solves erosion with no wider cost.
items.random_wrong: Sea walls work by freezing the waves.
items.why: Students treat defences as final fixes, missing knock-on effects within sediment cells.

items.q: Is the rate of coastal erosion determined by rock type alone?
items.correct: No — rates depend on lithology plus wave energy, fetch, beach width, subaerial weathering and human interference such as groynes trapping sediment updrift.
items.misconception: Soft rock automatically means rapid erosion regardless of other factors.
items.random_wrong: Erosion rates are set by local councils.
items.why: Multi-factor explanation distinguishes top answers on rapidly eroding coasts like Holderness.

items.q: Are eustatic and isostatic sea-level changes the same thing?
items.correct: No — eustatic change is a global shift in ocean water volume, while isostatic change is local land rising or sinking, such as post-glacial rebound; relative sea level combines both.
items.misconception: All sea-level change is the ocean itself rising.
items.random_wrong: Isostatic change is caused by the tides.
items.why: Separating global water volume from local land movement is essential for explaining emergent and submergent coasts.

items.q: What is post-glacial rebound an example of?
items.why: Correct answer: Isostatic. “Eustatic” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: 1.8m/year. “0.5m/year” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many sediment cells does England have?
items.why: The real figure is 11 cells. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
